name: Elsevier Inc. v. Meta Platforms, Inc.

docket_entries.raw_text: >>>NOTICE REGARDING PRO HAC VICE MOTION. Regarding Document No. 42 MOTION for Eli Goldman to Appear Pro Hac Vice . Filing fee $ 200.00, receipt number ANYSDC-32909786. Motion and supporting papers to be reviewed by Clerk's Office staff.. The document has been reviewed and there are no deficiencies. (bc)
docket_entries.summary: The clerk's office reviewed a motion for attorney Eli Goldman to appear pro hac vice and found no deficiencies in the filing.

docket_entries.raw_text: Set/Reset Hearings: Initial Conference set for 7/27/2026 at 10:00 AM before Judge P. Kevin Castel. (vfr)
docket_entries.summary: The court scheduled an initial conference for July 27, 2026 at 10:00 AM before Judge P. Kevin Castel.

docket_entries.raw_text: MOTION for Eli Goldman to Appear Pro Hac Vice . Filing fee $ 200.00, receipt number ANYSDC-32909786. Motion and supporting papers to be reviewed by Clerk's Office staff. Document filed by Cengage Learning, Inc., Elsevier Inc., Hachette Book Group, Inc., Macmillan Publishing Group, LLC d/b/a Macmillan Publishers, McGraw Hill LLC, S.C.R.I.B.E., Inc., Scott Turow. (Attachments: # 1 Exhibit Declaration of Eli Goldman, # 2 Exhibit A - Certificate of Good Standing, # 3 Proposed Order Granting Motion).(Goldman, Eli) (Entered: 05/28/2026)
docket_entries.summary: Multiple plaintiffs including Elsevier Inc. , Cengage Learning, Inc. , and other publishers filed a motion for attorney Eli Goldman to appear pro hac vice in their case against Meta Platforms. The motion includes Goldman's declaration, a certificate of good standing, and a proposed order, and is pending review by the Clerk's Office staff.

docket_entries.raw_text: ORDER granting 40 Letter Motion for Extension of Time to Answer re 1 Complaint. Time for all defendants to respond extended to July 13, 2026. Conference adjourned from June 29, 2026 to July 27, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. Call-In: 855-244-8681, Access Code: 2305 810 3970#. SO ORDERED. Meta Platforms, Inc. answer due 7/13/2026. (Signed by Judge P. Kevin Castel on 5/26/2026) (vfr) (Entered: 05/26/2026)
docket_entries.summary: The court granted Meta's motion to extend the deadline for answering Elsevier's complaint until July 13, 2026. The court also rescheduled a conference from June 29 to July 27, 2026.
